Requirement.Name: Requirement Typing Consistency
Requirement.Content: The system shall classify each requirement as either a Functional Requirement (FR) or a Non-Functional Requirement (NFR) in the <Type> tag and shall not use combined type labels such as "NFR / FR", thereby ensuring that the example requirement is treated purely as documentation and not implemented as a functional feature.

Requirement.Name: Autonomous Exploration Mode
Requirement.Content: The system shall provide an "Exploration" operating mode in which the robot shall autonomously navigate the maze without manual control input after a start command is issued.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Start Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall start the autonomous exploration mode only after receiving a start signal via a designated user interface or control input and confirming that all required sensors and actuators are in an operational state.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Termination Conditions
Requirement.Content: The system shall terminate exploration mode when at least one of the following conditions is met: (a) all reachable cells in the maze are mapped as free or blocked; (b) a predefined exploration time limit is reached; or (c) a stop command is received from the user interface.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Coverage Strategy
Requirement.Content: During exploration mode, the system shall apply a deterministic coverage strategy, such as flood-fill or frontier-based exploration, that repeatedly selects the next navigation target among currently reachable unknown cells until no such unknown cell remains.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Localization Update
Requirement.Content: During exploration, the system shall continuously estimate the robot's current pose (position and orientation) within the maze coordinate system using wheel odometry and, if available, additional sensors, and shall update this pose estimate at a fixed rate of at least 10 Hz.

Requirement.Name: Wall Presence Detection
Requirement.Content: The system shall detect the presence of a wall in front, left, and right of the robot by processing the configured distance or proximity sensors and shall provide a Boolean wall flag for each observed direction in the internal state at a minimum rate of 20 Hz.

Requirement.Name: Wall Distance Thresholds
Requirement.Content: The system shall treat any obstacle detected within a configurable safety distance threshold, expressed in centimeters, as a wall and shall store this threshold as a parameter that can be set at configuration time without code changes.

Requirement.Name: Collision-Free Motion Command Generation
Requirement.Content: The motion control subsystem shall generate translational and rotational velocity commands such that no commanded trajectory allows the robot's chassis to intersect a region classified as a wall in the internal map or currently detected as a wall by sensors.

Requirement.Name: Emergency Stop on Imminent Collision
Requirement.Content: The system shall issue an immediate stop command to the drive actuators whenever sensor data indicates that the measured distance to a wall in the direction of motion is less than a predefined emergency distance threshold.

Requirement.Name: Wall Avoidance Maneuver
Requirement.Content: When a wall is detected within the safety distance along the current heading, the system shall plan and execute an avoidance maneuver that either turns the robot in place or steers it toward a free neighboring cell while maintaining a non-collision trajectory.

Requirement.Name: Discrete Maze Grid Representation
Requirement.Content: The system shall represent the maze as a discrete grid of cells with a configurable cell size and a finite maximum grid dimension sufficient to cover the expected maze size.

Requirement.Name: Cell State Model
Requirement.Content: Each cell in the maze grid shall have a state field that shall be one of {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}, and the initial state of all cells shall be UNKNOWN before exploration starts.

Requirement.Name: Map Update from Sensor Observations
Requirement.Content: The system shall update the state of grid cells based on sensor observations and current pose, marking cells intersected by sensor beams with no detected wall as FREE and marking cells corresponding to detected walls as BLOCKED.

Requirement.Name: Consistency of Cell Classification
Requirement.Content: Once a cell is classified as BLOCKED, the system shall not reclassify it as FREE, and once a cell is classified as FREE, the system shall not reclassify it as BLOCKED unless marked as uncertain by a fault-tolerance mechanism handling sensor errors.

Requirement.Name: Map Origin and Start Cell
Requirement.Content: The system shall define the robot's initial position at the start of exploration as the origin of the maze coordinate system and shall store the corresponding grid cell index as the start cell.

Requirement.Name: Mapping Time Constraint Management
Requirement.Content: The system shall maintain an exploration timer starting at the beginning of exploration mode and shall ensure that the exploration termination logic is capable of stopping exploration such that the mapping process completes within 5 minutes of timer start under nominal conditions.

Requirement.Name: Time-Bounded Planning and Control Cycles
Requirement.Content: The system shall limit the execution time of exploration planning and control loop iterations so that the combined cycle time, including sensor processing, map update, path planning, and command output, does not exceed a configurable maximum period that supports completion of mapping within 5 minutes.

Requirement.Name: Grid-Based Path Planning
Requirement.Content: The system shall compute paths as sequences of adjacent FREE cells in the maze grid, treating BLOCKED cells as non-traversable and UNKNOWN cells as non-traversable for planned optimal runs.

Requirement.Name: Path Optimality Criterion
Requirement.Content: The system shall define path optimality in terms of minimum total path cost, where path cost is at least the sum of per-step costs proportional to the Euclidean or Manhattan distance between successive grid cells.

Requirement.Name: Shortest Path Algorithm
Requirement.Content: The system shall implement a deterministic graph search algorithm, such as Dijkstra or A*, to compute the minimal-cost path on the grid from a start cell to a target cell using the defined path cost metric.

Requirement.Name: Replanning Capability
Requirement.Content: The system shall support replanning of a path whenever the map is updated with new BLOCKED cells that intersect or invalidate the current planned path.

Requirement.Name: Path Representation for Controller
Requirement.Content: The system shall expose planned paths as ordered lists of waypoints in maze coordinates that can be consumed by the motion control subsystem to generate actuator commands.

Requirement.Name: Return to Start Trigger
Requirement.Content: After exploration has terminated, the system shall automatically initiate a "Return to Start" phase without requiring additional user input.

Requirement.Name: Path Planning to Start Cell
Requirement.Content: During the "Return to Start" phase, the system shall compute a path from the robot's current cell to the stored start cell using the optimal path planning algorithm on the final map.

Requirement.Name: Return Path Execution
Requirement.Content: The system shall command the actuators to follow the planned path back to the start cell while continuing to obey collision avoidance constraints.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Mode Activation
Requirement.Content: After the robot has returned to the start cell and is stationary, the system shall activate a "Second Run" mode that uses the stored maze map and planned optimal path to the target.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Start Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall start the second run either automatically after a configurable delay or upon reception of a dedicated start command from the user interface.

Requirement.Name: Most Efficient Path Selection
Requirement.Content: Before executing the second run, the system shall compute the minimal-cost path from the start cell to the target cell on the final map using the defined optimality criterion and shall select this path for execution.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Path Execution
Requirement.Content: During the second run, the system shall command the robot to follow the selected optimal path with speed and acceleration parameters that may differ from exploration mode but still respect collision avoidance and actuator constraints.

Requirement.Name: Target Cell Definition
Requirement.Content: The system shall define the target as a specific grid cell or set of cells identified during exploration, such as the goal cell detected by a goal marker, and shall store the target cell index for use in path planning.

Requirement.Name: Collision-Free State Invariant
Requirement.Content: The control logic shall be designed such that any normal operating state with non-zero forward velocity implies that the predicted motion for at least one control cycle period does not intersect any region classified as a wall by the map or sensors.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Plausibility Check
Requirement.Content: The system shall implement plausibility checks on sensor data, such as range limits and rate-of-change thresholds, and shall mark any readings that violate these checks as invalid.

Requirement.Name: Graceful Degradation on Sensor Fault
Requirement.Content: When a sensor is detected as faulty or its data is marked invalid, the system shall either switch to a fallback sensor or exploration behavior with more conservative motion limits or shall enter a safe stop state while signalling the fault condition.

Requirement.Name: Redundant Wall Estimation
Requirement.Content: The system shall, when multiple sensors observe the same maze region, combine their readings to determine wall presence using a rule such as majority voting or minimum distance to reduce the effect of faulty sensor data.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Acquisition
Requirement.Content: The system shall periodically read raw data from each sensor specified in the sensor data interface description at or above its specified minimum sampling rate.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Conversion
Requirement.Content: The system shall convert raw sensor data into standardized engineering units, such as centimeters for distance and degrees or radians for orientation, using calibration parameters defined in the sensor data interface description.

Requirement.Name: Timestamping of Sensor Readings
Requirement.Content: The system shall associate each sensor reading with a timestamp from a monotonic system clock to enable time-consistent fusion and processing.

Requirement.Name: Actuator Command Abstraction
Requirement.Content: The system shall represent actuator control commands using an abstract interface, such as desired linear and angular velocities or motor speed setpoints, that is mapped to the specific low-level motor command format defined in the motor interface description.

Requirement.Name: Actuator Command Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall send actuator control commands to the motors at a fixed control rate, configurable but not less than 20 Hz during motion.

Requirement.Name: Motor Command Limits
Requirement.Content: The system shall enforce configurable upper bounds on acceleration, deceleration, and maximum speed in the actuator commands so that motor commands remain within safe operating limits for the robot hardware.

Requirement.Name: Stop Command Interface
Requirement.Content: The system shall provide a dedicated command that immediately sets all drive motor speed references to zero and shall issue this command whenever an emergency stop or collision-imminent condition is detected.
